note_data.title: Population Structure
keyPoints: Rice is a kharif crop requiring warm temperatures for growth 20°C to 30°C and monsoon rain for growth/flooding fields 1270-2000mms
Wheat is a rabi crop requiring mild/warm temperature 10 degree – 20 degree for growing and 20 degrees for ripening
Cash crop farms use large fields with higher capital required for modern irrigation system i.e. perennial canals, tubewells, sprinklers
Subsistence farming utilizes small land with low capital invested because of poverty and old irrigation methods like shaduf
Barani farming depends entirely on rainfall/rain-fed area with low banks/bunds constructed and field size varies considerably
Natural inundation farming can only be carried out after flooding and farmers have no control of water supply with variable seasonal cropping
The two main tobacco growing districts in Pakistan are in NWFP: Mardan and Peshawar
Winter crop growing enables double or multiple crop, provides job throughout the year, and increases income of farmers
Rice is not grown in areas which only practise barani farming because it requires over 1275mm rainfall in growing season
Rabi crops require light rain of dec-march (western depression) to help in growth and dry period in may suitable for harvesting

definitions.definition: Crops grown for sale/profit/commercial rather than for consumption by the farmers
Growing for sale and profit purpose
Crops grown in warm season requiring temperatures 20°C to 30°C with monsoon rain
Farming that depends entirely on rainfall/rain-fed area with banks/bunds constructed

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Say what you see in the pyramid - wide/narrow base, bulges, male/female differences
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'

modelAnswer.question: Explain why countries with wide-based population pyramids face economic challenges. [6 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Countries with wide-based pyramids have high dependency ratios because there are many young dependents aged 0-14 who do not work. This means fewer people in the working population (15-64) must support more dependents, creating economic pressure. The government must spend more money on education and healthcare for the large young population, reducing funds available for economic development. There is high unemployment because many young people enter the job market each year but there are insufficient jobs available. Food security becomes an issue because more mouths need feeding but fewer people are economically productive. Additionally, these countries often have low GDP per capita because the large dependent population reduces overall economic output per person.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: High dependency ratios/many young dependents
Mark 2: Fewer workers support more dependents
Mark 6: Low GDP per capita/reduced economic output

commonMistakes: Confusing kharif and rabi seasons - rice is kharif (warm season) while wheat is rabi (cool season)
Not understanding that rice cannot be grown in barani areas due to insufficient rainfall requirements
Mixing up subsistence and cash crop farming characteristics and inputs
Forgetting that natural inundation farming has no control over water supply unlike irrigation farming
examinerTips: Remember specific temperature requirements: wheat needs 10-20°C for growing and 20°C for ripening
Include both human inputs and natural conditions when describing crop farming systems
State specific rainfall amounts - rice requires over 1275mm, wheat needs moderate rainfall 150mm-300mm
formulas: Dependency Ratio = (Young Dependents + Elderly Dependents) ÷ Working Population × 100
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Population pyramid with horizontal bars showing age groups on vertical axis (0-4, 5-9, etc.), males on left, females on right. Wide base = young population (LEDC), narrow base = ageing population (MEDC). Always label axes and include a key.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2019 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to describe three differences between the population structure of Western Europe and West Africa, focusing on age groups and dependency ratios
This appeared in O/N 2018 P2 Q2 (4 marks) — candidates had to describe differences between population structures of Asia and Africa using specific age categories
Featured in M/J 2023 P2 Q3 (6 marks) — students needed to describe three features of Japan's population structure typical of an MEDC, including dependency ratios and gender balance
Tested in O/N 2022 P2 Q5 (3 marks) — required identification of one problem arising from Central African Republic's population structure and its impacts

workedExample.question: Describe three features of Japan's population structure which are typical of an MEDC. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the three main age groups in population structure - young dependents (0-19), economically active (20-64), and old dependents (65+)
Step 2: Compare Japan's structure to typical MEDC patterns - low birth rates, low death rates, aging population
Step 3: Structure answer with one feature per paragraph, using specific age ranges and clear comparisons
Step 4: Include quantitative descriptions where possible (large/small proportions) rather than vague terms
workedExample.answer: Even gender balance with similar proportions of males and females across all age groups. Large proportion of old dependents over 65 years due to high life expectancy and low birth rates. Small proportion of young dependents aged 0-19 reflecting low birth rates typical of developed countries.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for each correctly identified feature (max 3). 1 mark for each appropriate explanation linking to MEDC characteristics (max 3). Accept: aging population, low dependency ratio, narrow base if explained.

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused age groups and wrote about 0-15 instead of 0-19' — always use the correct age boundaries: 0-19 (young dependents), 20-64 (economically active), 65+ (old dependents)

Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates often described population pyramids without relating features to MEDC/LEDC characteristics' — always link population structure features to development level and explain why they occur

Examiner Report 2021: 'Students frequently gave vague answers like "lots of old people" without using proper terminology' — use precise terms like 'high proportion of old dependents' and 'low dependency ratio' rather than casual language
note_data.keyDiagram: Population pyramid showing contrasting MEDC vs LEDC structures. MEDC pyramid: narrow base (low birth rate), wide middle section (large economically active population), broad top (many elderly). LEDC pyramid: wide base (high birth rate), narrow middle (smaller working population), very narrow top (few elderly). Include age groups 0-19, 20-64, 65+ clearly labeled, with male/female sides and percentage scales. Add arrows showing population flow and dependency ratios.
note_data.quickRecap: Pakistan grows both kharif crops (like rice) in warm seasons and rabi crops (like wheat) in cooler seasons. Cash crop farming uses modern inputs and large fields while subsistence farming relies on traditional methods and small plots. Different farming systems include irrigation, natural inundation, and barani (rainfall-dependent) farming, each with distinct characteristics and crop requirements.
